data.title: Russia Was Set For An Oil Windfall From US–Iran War — Then Ukrainian Drone Strikes Knocked Out Nearly 40% Of Its Export Capacity
data.description:
The U.S. war with Iran has led to a rise in oil prices, which was expected to provide a financial windfall for Russia. However, continuous Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia’s major export hubs have put a damper on these expectations.

When last checked, Brent crude oil was trading 2.89% higher at $108.46 per barrel.

Ukraine also carried out drone strikes on Russia's major export hubs, targeting Novorossiysk, Primorsk, and Ust-Luga ports, previously. Fresh Sunday attacks parked fires at the Ust-Luga port, as per Reuters.

Russian Oil Revenue Soars Amid Risks

However, Russian President Vladimir Putin urged Russian oil and gas companies to stay cautious and use rising hydrocarbon revenues to pay down debt rather than increase spending.

Earlier this month, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that global oil sanctions and strikes on Russian energy infrastructure have pushed Moscow's 2026 deficit past $100 billion. But he warned that recent gains from the Middle East war, about $10 billion in two weeks, could embolden Putin to continue the conflict.

Notably, Russia's economy has been strained since it invaded Ukraine in 2022, with sanctions, war-driven economic mobilization, labor shortages, and high inflation prompting the central bank to maintain elevated interest rates.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Crypto Treasury Firms Regain Balance—And Strategy, BitMine Keep Buying
data.description:
Grayscale says crypto treasury firms are likely to remain a permanent fixture of the investing landscape as they diversify into new business lines.

DATs Stabilized Through Strategic Approaches

In a post on March 26, Grayscale head of research Zach Pandl noted that DATs surged last year as strong valuations enabled aggressive token accumulation and inspired similar firms.

However, by late 2025, momentum weakened as many shares traded below their underlying crypto values, raising questions about sustainability and even prompting concerns that some firms could be excluded from benchmark indices.

More recently, DATs have regained stability by improving capital structures, generating yield through staking and decentralized finance.

Crypto Treasury Firms Still Accumulate

Despite earlier volatility, forced liquidations among DATs have remained rare, and many continue to accumulate digital assets. Grayscale said these points point to a maturing segment of the market that offers both benefits and trade-offs compared with crypto exchange-traded products.

Data from CoinGecko showed the total value of corporate crypto treasuries rose 1.8% over the past 24 hours to $99 billion.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Peloton Stock Breaks Above Key Resistance: What's Next For Peloton?
data.description:
Breakout Attempt Faces Key Test As Momentum Improves

A sustained move above $4.50 could open the door for further upside, while a move back below the level would suggest a failed breakout.

The recent move comes as shares climb 15.5% above the 20-day SMA, though they remain 19.2% below the 100-day SMA, reflecting a short-term bounce within a broader downtrend. The stock is also down 29.91% over the past 12 months and continues to trade closer to its 52-week lows than highs.

Momentum indicators show early signs of improvement. The RSI is at 57.12, sitting in neutral territory and suggesting the rebound isn't overstretched. Meanwhile, MACD is at -0.0948 versus a signal line at -0.1978, a bullish configuration that points to strengthening momentum even as the indicator remains below zero.

Taken together, the setup reflects improving short-term momentum within a still-weak longer-term structure.

Key support sits at $4.00.

Peloton Shares Edge Higher

PTON Price Action: At the time of publication, Peloton shares are trading 2.71% higher at $4.55, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Why Is Lantern Pharma Stock Soaring Monday?
data.description:
The company moved quickly to address what it termed “fabricated” information regarding its leadership.

Company Refutes False CEO Resignation

Lantern Pharma issued a statement late Friday to correct a third-party report. The report incorrectly claimed Panna Sharma had stepped down as President and CEO.

“This claim is false, entirely without basis, and appears designed to mislead investors,” the company stated. Lantern confirmed that Sharma remains fully engaged in his roles.

Board Maintains Full Confidence

The Board of Directors clarified they have not planned any leadership changes. Chairman Donald Jeff Keyser expressed “full confidence” in Sharma’s leadership. Keyser noted the company is investigating the origin of the article and potential coordinated trading activity.

Clinical Progress and Earnings Focus

The volatility overshadowed Friday’s news regarding Starlight Therapeutics. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration cleared an IND application for a Phase 1 pediatric clinical trial of STAR-001.

Investors now look toward the fourth quarter of 2025 earnings call on Monday.

Technical Analysis

Lantern Pharma is trading 47.1% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 59.1% below its 100-day SMA, keeping the trend firmly bearish across multiple timeframes.

Shares are down 68.36% over the past 12 months and are sitting much closer to the 52-week low of $1.11 than the 52-week high of $5.74.

The RSI is at 14.35, which is deeply oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is at -0.2684 versus a signal line at -0.1857.

  • Key Resistance: $2.50
  • Key Support: $1.00

LTRN Stock Price Activity: Lantern Pharma shares were up 11.61% at $1.25 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by Thapana_Studio via Shutterstock
